tokens: And, Dave, are you off the Rover, at the front of the Rover, now?

tokens: Joe, do you want to reset on the Nav system?

tokens: (Long Pause) Hey, Houston, with the attitude of the Rover (which is headed northwest), I'm having a tough time finding the Earth in the field-of-view because of the Sun shining in my eyes.

tokens: This is only going to be a short stop.
(Static) Do you think you can do without it (the TV) here?

tokens: We can certainly do without it, Dave.

tokens: Roger; and, Jim, perhaps you could get bag 2 on the right-hand side of the handtool carrier.

tokens: (Very faint signal) Okay, I'll be there pretty quick.

tokens: What are you going to use for the UHT?

tokens: You don't use the UHT that much.

tokens: Let's take that (yo-yo) back into the LM, and maybe we can repair it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, you want to open the gate (at the back of the Rover)?

tokens: Okay, if you'd just bend over right there, I can get the bag (Jim's SCB) off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here's a bag; want to hang onto it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I have bag 4 (which was Jim's) here, and I'll hang it temporarily on the right side (of the HTC), Joe.

tokens: Put it under the seat, Jim, and get bag 2...

tokens: Hey, we want to put bag 4 under your seat.

tokens: (After Dave removes SCB-4 from the HTC) I'll put bag 2 there (on the HTC).

tokens: (Pause) That (presumably SCB-1)'s mine, I'll get it.

tokens: You want to go ahead and (start the ALSEP off-load)?

tokens: I'll pick it up; remind me after I get the LR-cubed on the Rover, Joe; I'll do that.

tokens: (To Jim) The ALSEP's in a good place to get off, it looks like.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah; you got a good attitude there.

tokens: (Joking) Well, I was thinking of ALSEP (during the landing).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Straining as he pulls the lanyard that opens the doors) Okay, the doors are coming open.

tokens: Boy, it's going to be hard to keep the dust off the ALSEP.

tokens: Let me get out of your way.
Here is a nice spot up here to set them down.

tokens: Okay, give me one of those (UHTs)...if you can get them apart.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Never seen them wedged in there like that before.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Lot of surprises; got to expect surprises.

I've never seen it wedged in there like that either.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Straps do come handy for something, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, my package is a lot closer than normal, Jim, so watch out when you back up.

tokens: I'm going over to tip the fuel cask.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just want to empty all the loose gear - the extra gear - (reading) core tube and its cap, and SESC into bag number 2.

tokens: The core tube and its caps into bag number 2.

tokens: Okay, the unused core tube now goes into bag number 2.

tokens: And the SESC that's on my pack comes out of its pocket and it goes into bag number 2.

tokens: Okay, and the other number on that core tube (that Jim drove at Station 2) is 01, by the way.

tokens: Okay, the RTG is fueled, Joe.

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, the LR cubed is out.

tokens: And, Jim, just a reminder on (closing) those SEQ bay doors if you're getting ready to leave that area.

tokens: He's not going to forget that, Joe.

tokens: (Straining) Booms are out a little farther than normal.

tokens: (Straining) Okay, Jim, your seatbelt is going to have to get readjusted, because I want to make sure I get the LR-cubed tied down good here.

tokens: Okay; the SEQ Bay doors are closed, Joe.

tokens: I can't go fast carrying this.

tokens: When you climb on the Rover, I'd like a mark on that, and then I'd like to update you with some further EVA planning that's going on down here as you drive out to the ALSEP site.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Okay, Joe, on the Rover (Pause) And heading to the ALSEP site.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As you're moving out there, I guess I've got some good news and some bad news for you.
Your O2 usage rate has been considerably higher than we've been planning on.
It may cut the EVA a little short.
We want you to be aware of this right now, and we're watching it real close, and we'll advise you in real time.
It is good to note, though, that if you can do as little unnecessary moving around as possible, we may be able to run the EVA out to the full 7 hours.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll do my best (Pause) (Chuckling) You know, Joe, "little moving around" with the drill is...That's sort of hard.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That go for me too, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, you're a little low, but not...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...as low as Dave at the moment.
You're probably trying to get out of Station 8 already.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's find a spot out here, Jim.
There's just no place that's really nice and smooth.
(Pause) And the zero phase sure doesn't help anything.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I've got...This is probably as good as we're going to do.

tokens: And it's gently rolling, but I think we're all right.

tokens: And, Jim, you might want to take a short breather after carrying that heavy package.
